title: How Much Longer For This Crypto Winter?
text:
How Much Longer For This Crypto Winter?

Will Denyer

Gavekal Research | 24 Jul 2026

Imagine the US central bank promised to halve its inflation target every four years and abruptly adjusted monetary policy accordingly. Financial markets would begin to price in the telegraphed changes well in advance, though some of the effects would only be felt after the event. Momentum could easily push prices beyond what the policy change justified, setting the stage for an eventual correction. Of course, the Federal Reserve does not operate this way. But bitcoin does.It is known as the bitcoin halving cycle, and it appears to have been at least partly responsible for every crypto bull and bear market to date, including the current downturn. As such, it may offer clues as to how much longer the crypto winter is likely to last, and how much further bitcoin has to fall.
